heading: Start-up expenditures
label: 195 Start-up expenditures
notes: Amendments2025—Subsec. (c)(1). substituted “174, or 174A” for “or 174” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 119–212010—Subsec. (b)(3). added par. (3).Pub. L. 111–2402004—Subsec. (b). , substituted “deduct” for “amortize” in heading.Pub. L. 108–357, § 902(a)(2)Subsec. (b)(1). , amended heading and text of par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Start-up expenditures may, at the election of the taxpayer, be treated as deferred expenses. Such deferred expenses shall be allowed as a deduction prorated equally over such period of not less than 60 months as may be selected by the taxpayer (beginning with the month in which the active trade or business begins).”Pub. children.heading: Capitalization of expenditures
children.label: a Capitalization of expenditures
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, no deduction shall be allowed for start-up expenditures.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of start-up expenditures with respect to the active trade or business, or

children.label: ii
children.text: $5,000, reduced (but not below zero) by the amount by which such start-up expenditures exceed $50,000, and

children.label: B
children.text: the remainder of such start-up expenditures shall be allowed as a deduction ratably over the 180-month period beginning with the month in which the active trade or business begins.

children.heading: Dispositions before close of amortization period
children.label: 2 Dispositions before close of amortization period
children.text: In any case in which a trade or business is completely disposed of by the taxpayer before the end of the period to which paragraph (1) applies, any deferred expenses attributable to such trade or business which were not allowed as a deduction by reason of this section may be deducted to the extent allowable under section 165.

children.heading: Special rule for taxable years beginning in 2010
children.label: 3 Special rule for taxable years beginning in 2010
i
children.text: investigating the creation or acquisition of an active trade or business, or

children.label: ii
children.text: creating an active trade or business, or

children.label: iii
children.text: any activity engaged in for profit and for the production of income before the day on which the active trade or business begins, in anticipation of such activity becoming an active trade or business, and

children.label: B
children.text: which, if paid or incurred in connection with the operation of an existing active trade or business (in the same field as the trade or business referred to in subparagraph (A)), would be allowable as a deduction for the taxable year in which paid or incurred.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the determination of when an active trade or business begins shall be made in accordance with such regulations as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Acquired trade or business
children.label: B Acquired trade or business
children.text: An acquired active trade or business shall be treated as beginning when the taxpayer acquires it.

children.heading: Time for making election
children.label: 1 Time for making election
children.text: An election under subsection (b) shall be made not later than the time prescribed by law for filing the return for the taxable year in which the trade or business begins (including extensions thereof).

children.heading: Scope of election
children.label: 2 Scope of election
children.text: The period selected under subsection (b) shall be adhered to in computing taxable income for the taxable year for which the election is made and all subsequent taxable years.
